title: Sunrun's Storage Attachment Rate Reaches 62% in Q4 2024
text: Sunrun concluded Q4 2024 with energy storage attachment rate of 62%, up from 60% in previous quarter, representing the company's continued emphasis on storage-first strategy. Storage adoption rates vary significantly by geography with Hawaii and Puerto Rico leading at nearly 100%, California at approximately 89%, and Texas at 66%, while remainder of country remained at just 10% during Q4 installations. Solar photovoltaic additions grew 6% year-over-year to 242MW in Q4 while energy storage capacity jumped 78% year-over-year with 392MWh added. By year-end 2024, Sunrun accumulated 7.5GW of installed solar PV and 2.5GWh of storage capacity. The installer generated $34 million in positive cash flow during Q4 2024, marking third consecutive quarter of cash generation with management projecting this will continue throughout 2025. Sunrun operates 16 grid service programs across nine states with over 20,000 participating customers with virtual power plants achieving combined peak of nearly 80 megawatts during 2024, stated CEO Mary Powell. For Q1 2025, company targets 170-180MW of solar installations and 265-275MWh of storage capacity with full-year 2025 solar volume expected to remain flat year-over-year while storage projected to grow 30%.
